A.label: Fast lane
A.desc: Small, fully GPU-resident, sub-50 ms latency

B.label: Balanced
B.desc: Fits in 16 GB VRAM, the workhorse zone

C.label: Quality
C.desc: CPU offload, slow but highest accuracy

models.name: Qwen 3.5 4B
models.primary_use: ★ Incumbent fast lane — OpenClaw routing, fast tool calls, edge
models.note: Feb 2026 release, swapped into agentic_light on 2026-05-10. Hybrid Gated DeltaNet + sparse MoE on a 4B base, 262k native ctx, 201 languages incl. ES/CA. No new 4B-class challenger through July 2026.

models.name: Qwen3 4B (2507)
models.note: Solid small agentic model from late 2025. Dominated by Qwen 3.5 4B on context, multilingual reach, and quality.

models.name: Gemma 3 4B
models.primary_use: Fast multilingual triage, transcript shaping, Catalan
models.note: Best Catalan support of the small models. Weaker function calling than Qwen3.5. Useful as a CPU-side translation specialist on the 7800X3D.

models.name: Phi-4 Mini
models.note: Speed king in the small tier, but multilingual gap rules it out for ES/CA.

models.name: Llama 3.2 3B
models.note: Dominated. 2026 community testing reports unreliable triage / hallucinated replies.

models.name: Granite 4.1 8B
models.note: Late-Apr 2026 release; strong enterprise tool-calling in the 8B class (est. composite — headline numbers are coding-weighted, which this stack ignores). Dominated by Qwen 3.5 4B on speed and Qwen3.5 9B on quality at similar VRAM.

models.name: Ministral 3 14B
models.note: Dec 2025 release. Good European multilingual incl. ES, 256k ctx — but dominated by GPT-OSS 20B and the tier-B MoEs on both chart axes.

models.name: Qwen3.5 9B
models.note: Confirmed 250k+ context on this exact GPU. Loses to Gemma 4 26B MoE and Qwen 3.6 35B-A3B on speed AND quality.

models.name: Gemma 4 12B Unified
models.note: Released 2026-06-03. Encoder-free multimodal: text, image, and native AUDIO input straight into the backbone; 140 languages; benchmark coverage 'nearing the 26B' (est. composite 76, tps est. from Gemma 3 12B). Dominated on both chart axes by Gemma 4 26B MoE — its niche is the ~7 GB footprint and native-audio input, neither of which the current roles need. Watch for a future combined ASR+LLM lane.

models.name: Gemma 3 12B
models.note: Dominated by Gemma 4 26B MoE on every axis, and by Gemma 4 12B Unified within its own size class.

models.name: Mistral Small 3.2
models.note: Strong European multilingual but dominated on speed by MoE alternatives.

models.name: GPT-OSS 20B
models.note: Excellent BFCL/τ-bench, native MXFP4 fits 16 GB. MMMLU Spanish 79.7 (medium reasoning) confirmed this run — decent, not leading. Catalan still unverified.

models.name: Gemma 4 26B MoE
models.primary_use: ★ Incumbent workhorse — transcripts, documents, deep agentic, EN↔ES↔CA
models.note: Still the standout 16 GB-class model as of July 2026. Tie with Qwen 3.6 35B-A3B persists; Kimi K3 (2.8T, Jul 16) and GLM-5.2 (744B) both confirmed hardware NO-GOs this run, Mistral's 'fat but sparse' MoE still pre-open-weights.

models.name: Qwen 3.6 35B-A3B
models.primary_use: ★ Tied with Gemma 4 26B MoE — agentic, long-doc, 201-language multilingual
models.note: Released 2026-04-16; still the newest locally-runnable Qwen as of July 2026 — Qwen 3.7 confirmed API-only (Max/Plus), no open weights of any 3.7 variant found. Composite within ~1% of Gemma 4 26B → tie stands.

models.name: Qwen 3.6 27B (dense)
models.note: Dominated by the 35B-A3B MoE sibling on speed AND quality at the same VRAM footprint — MoE wins on this hardware.

models.name: Gemma 3 27B (dense)
models.note: Dominated by Gemma 4 26B MoE: same family, higher quality, ~6× faster decode thanks to MoE.

models.name: Qwen3.5-35B-A3B
models.note: Predecessor to Qwen 3.6 35B-A3B. Dominated by the 3.6 update on speed and benchmark scores at lower VRAM.

models.name: Qwen3 32B (dense)
models.primary_use: Quality runs, hard agentic, batch overnight
models.note: BFCL v3 leader among open weights (75.7%). ~3.5 GB CPU spill, ~10–12 t/s. Held off the Tier B frontier by Qwen 3.6 35B-A3B which matches its quality at ~9× the speed.

models.name: Mistral Medium 3.5
models.note: Dense 128B is too large for this box even with 128 GB RAM offload — single-digit t/s. Modified MIT license has revenue gates. Dropped on hardware grounds, not quality.

models.name: Llama 3.3 70B
models.note: Heavy CPU offload (24 GB+ in RAM). Dominated by Qwen3 32B: faster AND higher quality at lower VRAM.

models.name: Kimi K3
models.note: Announced 2026-07-16, weights ship 2026-07-27. Moonshot's own guidance: '64+ accelerators'. Even a 2-bit dynamic quant is ~700 GB — an order of magnitude past this box's ~144 GB combined VRAM+RAM. Not chart-plotted at a realistic operating point; listed for completeness only.

models.name: GLM-5.2
models.note: Standing NO-GO carried from prior runs (docs/glm-5.2-evaluation.md, #141): >1 TB VRAM in BF16, smallest useful quant ~245 GB combined vs this box's ~144 GB.

asr_models.name: Parakeet TDT v3 (Mac ANE)
asr_models.tagline: ★ New primary as of 2026-07-22 — placement/latency swap, not accuracy
asr_models.note: Became the audio_transcribe primary via #350 (fleet placement, #343 benchmark), with whisper as automatic failover (#348). Fastest STT in the fleet — 65.8× RTFx, sub-second even on 108 s clips. Published MLS/FLEURS Spanish WER modestly beats Whisper Large v3 (4.39% vs 4.89% MLS; 3.41% vs 4.32% FLEURS) — but this project's own jargon-heavy dictation-domain testing (docs/parakeet-asr-evaluation.md) shows the reverse: 2-4x worse WER, drops the 'Claude Code' wake phrase entirely, mangles 'YOLO'->'yellow'. FluidAudio's unintegrated CustomVocabularyContext rescorer is the identified fix — this run's runtime_upgrade recommendation.

asr_models.name: Whisper Large v3 Turbo (failover)
asr_models.tagline: ◆ Automatic failover since 2026-07-22 (was sole default through 2026-07-12)
asr_models.note: Demoted from primary to automatic failover by #350/#348. Still the accuracy leader on this domain thanks to the #91 --carry-initial-prompt boosting glossary. Now hosted on gaming (moved off tower to free GPU for agentic roles, #343) with a CPU fallback on the tower per #342.

asr_models.name: faster-whisper Turbo (CTranslate2)
asr_models.tagline: ✗ Disproven locally (#274) — no longer the primary's concern
asr_models.note: Measured 1.0x speedup (not the claimed ~2x), WER parity-to-worse, drops the 'Claude Code' wake phrase 0/2 across an 18-attempt sweep. Carried watch per docs/frontier/local-findings.md (#277). Now relevant only to the whisper failover leg's own engine, not the live primary.

asr_models.name: faster-whisper Large v3
asr_models.tagline: ☆ Use for ES → EN single-pass translation
asr_models.note: Higher accuracy than Turbo and supports task=translate. Fallback for workload G when the LLM slot is busy.

asr_models.name: IBM Granite Speech 3.3 8B
asr_models.tagline: ☆ Accuracy upgrade if the failover's own errors start to matter
asr_models.note: 5.85% avg WER — top tier on the HF leaderboard. EN/FR/DE/ES/PT plus speech translation X<->EN. Heavier (~5 GB, ~3x the latency).

asr_models.name: Qwen3-ASR 1.7B
asr_models.note: Technical report published Feb 2026 (arXiv 2601.21337) with multilingual evals on Common Voice/FLEURS/MLS — no head-to-head vs whisper on THIS project's jargon-heavy dictation domain, the exact gap that made Parakeet's generic leaderboard numbers misleading (see 7.2.1). Smoke-test locally before trusting any published number for this workload.

asr_models.name: NVIDIA Canary-Qwen 2.5B
asr_models.note: #1 on HF Open ASR Leaderboard at 5.63% WER, but English-strong with very limited Spanish support.

asr_translation_paths.label: Architecture (i) — Single-model
asr_translation_paths.headline: faster-whisper Large v3 with task=translate
asr_translation_paths.pros: Simplest pipeline, ~2 GB VRAM, ~30× realtime, single pass
asr_translation_paths.cons: Literal English output. No style polish, no summarization, occasional idiom loss.
asr_translation_paths.verdict: Fallback when LLM slot is occupied

asr_translation_paths.label: Architecture (ii) — Two-stage
asr_translation_paths.headline: faster-whisper Turbo (ES) → Gemma 4 26B MoE (translate + polish + disfluency)
asr_translation_paths.pros: Higher quality, context-aware translation, polishing + filler removal in same LLM call. Catalan extension comes free.
asr_translation_paths.cons: ~15 GB VRAM total — fast-lane model drops out for batches.

disfluency_verdict.decision: Fold into the LLM polishing pass
disfluency_verdict.reasoning: Specialized disfluency-removal models (DisfluencyFixer, DISCO) are research-grade and primarily EN/HI/DE/FR — Spanish coverage is sparse. Tier-B LLMs (Gemma 4 26B MoE, Qwen 3.6 35B-A3B) handle disfluency removal extremely well as part of an instruction-following pass. Adding a separate component means another model loaded, another VRAM slot, another point of failure, and worse multilingual coverage than the LLM you already run.
disfluency_verdict.prompt:
Clean up this transcript:
- Remove filler words (um, uh, eh, este, like, you know)
- Remove false starts and self-corrections, keep the corrected version
- Tighten conciseness without losing meaning or voice
- Preserve technical terms and proper nouns exactly
- Keep the output in [Spanish/English] — do not translate

Transcript:
{transcript}

concurrency_recipes.label: Recipe 1 — Two lanes
concurrency_recipes.note: Both at full speed. Total ~17 GB — minor shared-memory spill, handled gracefully on Blackwell. Tower GPU is exclusively agentic since the 2026-07-22 audio placement move (#343).

concurrency_recipes.label: Recipe 2 — Qwen 3.6 stack (Apache 2.0 only)
concurrency_recipes.note: All-Apache stack — useful when license clarity matters (Macaya Bank work). Speed parity with Recipe 1.

concurrency_recipes.label: Recipe 3 — Quality batch
concurrency_recipes.summary: Qwen 3.5 4B (GPU) + Qwen3 32B dense (GPU + ~3.5 GB CPU spill).
concurrency_recipes.note: Fast lane unaffected. 32B at ~10 t/s — overnight transcript reprocessing.

concurrency_recipes.label: Recipe 4 — Three concurrent
concurrency_recipes.note: The 7800X3D's 96 MB L3 makes Gemma 3 4B on CPU run ~10 t/s — useful as a Catalan-strong specialist.

top_picks.why: Best small agentic model on this hardware as of July 2026 — and now the incumbent. Hybrid DeltaNet + sparse MoE, 262k native ctx, 201 languages, Apache 2.0. No new 4B-class challenger since February.

99 t/s, full 256k context, Google-grade multilingual including Catalan. Tie with Qwen 3.6 35B-A3B persists through July 2026 — keep Gemma as default on Catalan track record; switch to Qwen 3.6 if you need pure Apache 2.0.
BFCL leader at 75.7%. ~10 t/s with mild CPU spill — reserve for batch quality runs where correctness > latency.

agentic_light.reason: Incumbent (swapped in 2026-05-10) is still the Tier A Pareto pick: ~110 t/s, ~3 GB, 262k native ctx, 201 languages, Apache 2.0. No new 4B-class entrant since February; Kimi K3, GLM-5.2, and Mistral's 'fat but sparse' MoE are all the wrong size class.

agentic_heavy.reason: Incumbent and Tier B top pick; tie (≤3%) with Qwen 3.6 35B-A3B persists. Kimi K3 (2.8T, Jul 16) and GLM-5.2 (744B) both confirmed hardware NO-GOs this run; Mistral's 'fat but sparse' MoE is early-access only, no open weights; Qwen 3.7 confirmed API-only.

audio_transcribe.reason: Incumbent changed out-of-band on 2026-07-22 (#350, voice-transcriber#149) — a latency/placement swap (#343 benchmark: 65.8x RTFx on Mac ANE), not an accuracy one. First-party testing (docs/parakeet-asr-evaluation.md, docs/voice-benchmark-2026-07.md) shows the swap drops the 'Claude Code' wake phrase and mangles jargon on this project's domain, despite Parakeet winning on generic published Spanish WER leaderboards (MLS/FLEURS). FluidAudio's CustomVocabularyContext rescorer is a real, already-identified, unintegrated fix. The whisper failover leg's own faster-whisper (CT2) engine swap stays disproven/watch per local-findings.md (#277).

audio_translate.reason: Unchanged: the two-stage path (Turbo → Gemma 4 26B MoE) stays the default for ES→EN; whisper-medium remains a lazy-loaded fallback for when the LLM slot is busy. No new speech-translation entrant this window changes this.
